memoryArtifacts.headline: William Gascoigne I and Elizabeth Mowbray
William Gascoigne I Chief Justice and wife Elizabeth Mowbray

popularStory.headline: William Gascoigne Bio (According to WIki)
popularStory.description:
Sir William Gascoigne (c. 1350 – 17 December 1419) was Chief Justice of England during the reign of King Henry IV.

Life and work

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Gascoigne I於大約1350年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親William Gascoigne Sr.43歲，母親Agnes Frank26歲。他於大約1386年與Elizabeth Mowbray在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1401年在英國被列為lord chief justice。他去世於從1419年12月14日到1419年12月23日在英國越可爾希亞活，埋葬在英國越可爾希亞活。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Gascoigne I于大约1350年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲William Gascoigne Sr.43岁，母亲Agnes Frank26岁。他于大约1386年与Elizabeth Mowbray在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1401年在英国被列为lord chief justice。他去世于从1419年12月14日到1419年12月23日在英国越可爾希亞活，埋葬在英国越可爾希亞活。

nameMeanings.name: Gascoigne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Gascon, Middle English Gascoun, Gascon ‘Gascon, native of Gascony’ or the adjective Gascuinz ‘of Gascony’. The name of the region derives from that of the Basques, who are found close by and formerly extended into this region as well; they are first named in Roman sources as Vascōnes, but the original meaning of the name derived from a root eusk- in the non-Indo-European language that they still speak today, is completely obscure. By the Middle Ages the Basques had been displaced from most of Gascony by speakers of Gascon (a dialect of Occitan, related to French), who were proverbial for their boastfulness. In the 11th century Gascony united with Aquitaine and was thus held by England between 1154 and 1453.

variants.name: Gascon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jaime, Mario, Miguel, Rafael, Ruben, Amado, Ana, Ana Maria, Angel, Caridad.  French Andre, Chantal, Jean-Guy, Jean-Philippe, Monique, Serge, Simonne.
French; Spanish (Gascón): habitational name for someone from the province of Gascony, Old French Gascogne (see Gascoigne ). This surname is also found in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Gascoigne I was born about 1350, in Yorkshire, England, his father, William Gascoigne Sr., was 43 and his mother, Agnes Frank, was 26. He married Elizabeth Mowbray about 1386, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1401, at the age of 52, his occupation is listed as lord chief justice in England. He died from 14 December 1419 to 23 December 1419, in Harewood, Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Harewood, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Gascoigne I est né environ 1350, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William Gascoigne Sr., avait 43 ans et sa mère, Agnes Frank, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Mowbray environ 1386, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1401, à l’âge de 52 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est lord chief justice à Angleterre. Il est décédé du 14 décembre 1419 au 23 décembre 1419, à Harewood, Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Harewood, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Gascoigne I um 1350 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Gascoigne Sr. 43 und seine Mutter Agnes Frank 26. Er hat um 1386 in Yorkshire, England Elizabeth Mowbray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1401, als er 52 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als lord chief justice in England aufgeführt. Er starb von 14. Dezember 1419 bis 23. Dezember 1419 in Harewood, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Harewood, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Gascoigne I è nato il circa 1350 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, William Gascoigne Sr., aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Frank, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Mowbray circa 1386, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1401, all'età di 52 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord chief justice nel England. Morì da 14 dicembre 1419 a 23 dicembre 1419, nel Harewood, Yorkshire, England, e fu sepolto a Harewood, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Gascoigne Iが約1350年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父William Gascoigne Sr.は43歳，母Agnes Frankは26歳であった。約1386年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでElizabeth Mowbrayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1401年，52歳の時，イギリスでの職業はlord chief justiceと記録されている。彼はから1419年12月14日まで1419年12月23日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハーエウッドで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアハーエウッドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Gascoigne I(이)가 대략 1350년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gascoigne Sr.(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Frank(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1386년 영국 요르크실르에서 Elizabeth Mowbray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1401년, 52세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국에서 lord chief justice이었다. 그는 1419년 12월 14일부터 1419년 12월 23일까지 영국 요르크실르 하레우드에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 하레우드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Gascoigne I nasceu aproximadamente 1350, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Gascoigne Sr., tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Frank, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Mowbray aproximadamente 1386, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1401, com 52 anos, sua profissão aparece como lord chief justice em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu de 14 de dezembro de 1419 a 23 de dezembro de 1419, em Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Gascoigne I родился около 1350 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Gascoigne Sr. было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Frank, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mowbray около 1386 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1401 году, в возрасте 52 лет,  он работал по профессии lord chief justice в местности Англия. Он умер с 14 декабря 1419 по 23 декабря 1419 в местности харюуд, еркшир, Англия и был похоронен в местности харюуд, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Gascoigne I nació aproximadamente 1350, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Gascoigne Sr., tenía 43 años y su mamá, Agnes Frank, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Mowbray aproximadamente 1386, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1401, a la edad de 52 años, su ocupación registrada era lord chief justice en Inglaterra. Murió desde 14 de diciembre de 1419 hasta 23 de diciembre de 1419, en Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Harewood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
